# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

def call(String gitUrl, String branchName = 'master', String registryURL='index.docker.io/library') {
  // Declare the stage for cloning source code
  stage('Clone GitHub Repository'){
    git url: gitUrl, branch: branchName
  }
  
  // Declare the stage for building Docker image
  stage('Build Docker Image'){
    withDockerContainer(image:'docker:dind',args:"-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock") {
      dir('path/to/dockerfile/directory') {  // replace path to your Dockerfile directory
        script{
          imageTag = buildArg1   // Build arguments should be passed from Jenkins UI for 'buildArg1' (image tag) and 'registryURL' (Docker registry URL)
          registryURI = buildArg2    // Use Groovy String manipulation to create docker login command with the URI provided. Replace "<username>:<password>" with your Docker credentials
          writeFile file:'docker_login', text:"echo '${registryCredentials}' | docker login ${registryURI} -u <username> --password-stdin"   // Write a shell script to log in into the registry. Replace "<username>:<password>" with your Docker credentials
          sh('bash docker_login')  // Run the written login shell script
        }
      }
      sh("docker build -t ${registryURL}/myImageName:${imageTag} --build-arg REGISTRY=${registryURI} .")   // Replace "myImageName" with your image name and replace the period '.' at end with path to Dockerfile
    }
  }
  
  // Declare the stage for pushing Docker Image
  stage('Push Docker Image') {
      withDockerContainer(image:'docker:dind',args:"-v /var/run<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>r.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"){
          sh("docker push ${registryURL}/myImageName:${imageTag}")   // Replace "myImageName" with your image name and replace the period '.' at end with path to Dockerfile
      }
  }
}
